channel.title: The Somerville Record
channel.description: The City Council and its committees, readable. Built from the public record.

item.title: File 26-1215 — Communications & Public Engagement Department applying for a Public Event License for Civic Day on September 10 from 3pm
item.description: Communications & Public Engagement Department applying for a Public Event License for Civic Day on September 10 from 3pm to 7:30pm.

item.title: File 26-1216 — Office of Immigrant Affairs applying for a Public Event License for Somerville Nepali Street Food Festival on September
item.description: Office of Immigrant Affairs applying for a Public Event License for Somerville Nepali Street Food Festival on September 5 from 11:30am to 9:30pm (raindate September 6).

item.description: City Council Chamber and Remote Participation

item.description: Public Library applying for a Public Event License for Comixcon 2026: Game On! on July 25 from 9am to 5pm (raindate August 1).

item.title: File 26-1190 — Reaffirming this City Council's recognition of July as Disability Pride Month in light of the recent Supreme Court rulin
item.description: Reaffirming this City Council's recognition of July as Disability Pride Month in light of the recent Supreme Court rulings and legal memorandum on the Olmstead decision from the Department of Justice.

item.title: File 26-1191 — In support of State Auditor Diana DiZoglio in her efforts to audit the General Court.
item.description: In support of State Auditor Diana DiZoglio in her efforts to audit the General Court.

item.description: Requesting approval of the Surveillance Technology Impact Report for Body Worn Cameras.

item.description: Commending Jenneen Pagliaro on the auspicious occasion of her retirement after over 38 years of dedicated service.

item.description: Approval of the Minutes of the Regular Meeting of June 11, 2026.

item.title: File 26-1199 — Requesting the appropriation of $204,400 from the Salary Contingency Salaries Account to the Department of Public Works
item.description: Requesting the appropriation of $204,400 from the Salary Contingency Salaries Account to the Department of Public Works School Custodians Personal Services Account to fund a Memorandum of Agreement with the Service Employees International Union Chapter 3 - School Custodians.

item.title: File 26-1158 — That the Strategy and Development Department and all relevant city departments conduct an economic analysis on the poten
item.description: That the Strategy and Development Department and all relevant city departments conduct an economic analysis on the potential impact to the city's finances, including investigating all potential service providers, the impact of the city's bond rating and the cost of enforcing the proposed Ordinance a

item.title: File 26-1159 — Eversource applying for a Grant of Location to install 7 feet of conduit in Wheatland Street, from utility pole 141/2 to
item.description: Eversource applying for a Grant of Location to install 7 feet of conduit in Wheatland Street, from utility pole 141/2 to a point of pickup at 6 Wheatland Street.

item.title: File 26-1160 — Eversource applying for a Grant of Location to install 4.5 feet of conduit in Line Street, from utility pole 211/7 to a
item.description: Eversource applying for a Grant of Location to install 4.5 feet of conduit in Line Street, from utility pole 211/7 to a point of pickup at 62 Line Street.

item.title: File 26-1161 — Eversource applying for a Grant of Location to install a total of 199 feet of conduit in Elm Street, from utility pole 1
item.description: Eversource applying for a Grant of Location to install a total of 199 feet of conduit in Elm Street, from utility pole 112/31 to manhole 18932, and additional conduit from manhole 18932 to a point of pickup at 199 Elm Street.

item.description: Arts Council applying for a Public Event License for Global Grooves: Rhythms of the Game on July 11 from 10am to 6pm (raindate July 12).

item.description: Mutual Aid Medford and Somerville applying for a Public Event License for Mutual Aid Medford and Somerville Free Clothing Store on August 22 from 7:30am to 4pm (raindate August 23).

item.title: File 26-1156 — Requesting authorization to borrow $130,392,395 in a bond, and to appropriate the same amount to participate in the Clea
item.description: Requesting authorization to borrow $130,392,395 in a bond, and to appropriate the same amount to participate in the Clean Water State Revolving Fund for the Morrison Avenue Linear Storage project.

item.title: File 26-1151 — Requesting the appropriation of $376,778,493 to fund the Fiscal Year 2027 General Fund Operating Budget.
item.description: Requesting the appropriation of $376,778,493 to fund the Fiscal Year 2027 General Fund Operating Budget.

item.title: 2026-06-25 — City Council
item.description: City Council Chamber and Remote Participation

item.title: File 26-1131 — Somerville Chamber of Commerce applying for a Public Event License for Somerville Watch Party on July 7 from 10am to 10p
item.description: Somerville Chamber of Commerce applying for a Public Event License for Somerville Watch Party on July 7 from 10am to 10pm.

item.title: File 26-1132 — That the funding for the previously cut Home Visitor position within the SomerBaby Program be reinstated through Health
item.description: That the funding for the previously cut Home Visitor position within the SomerBaby Program be reinstated through Health and Human Services and be inclusive of postpartum care.

item.title: File 26-1133 — That the Administration allocate $20,000 to provide additional out-of-school-time programming for teens on early-release
item.description: That the Administration allocate $20,000 to provide additional out-of-school-time programming for teens on early-release Wednesdays.

item.title: File 26-1134 — That the crossing guards receive a 10% raise in Fiscal Year 2027 budget.
item.description: That the crossing guards receive a 10% raise in Fiscal Year 2027 budget.

item.title: File 26-1135 — That the Administration restore the funding for the cut positions in the Racial and Social Justice Department and restor
item.description: That the Administration restore the funding for the cut positions in the Racial and Social Justice Department and restore funding towards the mission of the department.

item.title: File 26-1136 — That the Administration utilize the Small Business Stabilization Fund in Fiscal Year 2027 to infuse spending to local sm
item.description: That the Administration utilize the Small Business Stabilization Fund in Fiscal Year 2027 to infuse spending to local small businesses and educate them on how to use these funds.

item.title: File 26-1137 — That the collective salaries of Licensing Commissioners be increased to $15,180 with the Chair receiving $5,180 and Lice
item.description: That the collective salaries of Licensing Commissioners be increased to $15,180 with the Chair receiving $5,180 and Licensing Commissioners 2 and 3 receiving $5,000 each annually.

item.title: File 26-1138 — That the Administration add $400,000 in funding for Youth Development Services grants so that Teen Empowerment may have
item.description: That the Administration add $400,000 in funding for Youth Development Services grants so that Teen Empowerment may have full funding.

item.title: File 26-1141 — Commending Regina Bertholdo on the auspicious occasion of her retirement.
item.description: Commending Regina Bertholdo on the auspicious occasion of her retirement.

item.title: 2026-06-24 — Licenses and Permits Committee
item.description: Virtual

item.title: File 26-1121 — That the Director of the Office of Sustainability and Environment consider measures to reduce heat island effects on and
item.description: That the Director of the Office of Sustainability and Environment consider measures to reduce heat island effects on and around the grounds of the East Somerville Community School and the Edgerly School, including working with Open Spaces to add trees and green space.

item.title: File 26-1123 — Conveying a request regarding body-worn cameras.
item.description: Conveying a request regarding body-worn cameras.

item.title: File 26-1128 — Call of the Roll.
item.description: Call of the Roll.

item.title: File 26-1124 — Remembrances.
item.description: Remembrances.

item.title: File 26-1125 — Approval of the Minutes of the Regular Meeting of May 28, 2026.
item.description: Approval of the Minutes of the Regular Meeting of May 28, 2026.

item.title: File 26-1126 — Approval of the Minutes of the Special Meeting of June 4, 2026.
item.description: Approval of the Minutes of the Special Meeting of June 4, 2026.

item.title: File 26-1127 — Requesting the appropriation of $78,900 from the Medical Marijuana Stabilization Fund to fund a Jail Diversion Clinician
item.description: Requesting the appropriation of $78,900 from the Medical Marijuana Stabilization Fund to fund a Jail Diversion Clinician in the Community Outreach, Help and Recovery Unit.

item.title: File 26-1129 — Requesting the approval of a transfer of $76.96 from the Department of Veterans Services Professional & Technical Servic
item.description: Requesting the approval of a transfer of $76.96 from the Department of Veterans Services Professional & Technical Services Account to the Overtime Account to cover an expected year-end deficit.

item.title: File 26-1130 — Requesting approval to accept and expend a $14,974.64 grant with no match required, from the Massachusetts Department of
item.description: Requesting approval to accept and expend a $14,974.64 grant with no match required, from the Massachusetts Department of Public Health to the Health and Human Services Department for Local Public Health Funding for FIFA 2026 World Cup Community Events.

item.title: 2026-06-23 — Finance Committee
item.description: City Council Chamber

item.title: File 26-1111 — That Chapter 3, Article VIII, Division 1 of the Code of Ordinances be amended as detailed within.
item.description: That Chapter 3, Article VIII, Division 1 of the Code of Ordinances be amended as detailed within.

item.title: File 26-1112 — Calling for traffic calming measures in the Prospect Hill neighborhood.
item.description: Calling for traffic calming measures in the Prospect Hill neighborhood.

item.title: File 26-1113 — City Clerk requesting elimination of the Assistant City Clerk-Vital Records & Licensing position.
item.description: City Clerk requesting elimination of the Assistant City Clerk-Vital Records & Licensing position.

item.title: File 26-1114 — Requesting approval of a transfer of $595,106.69 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Capital Projects Engineeri
item.description: Requesting approval of a transfer of $595,106.69 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Capital Projects Engineering & Architecture Account to the Facility Renovation Stabilization Fund for continuing building design services.

item.title: File 26-1115 — Requesting approval of a transfer of $117,928 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Capital Projects Professional
item.description: Requesting approval of a transfer of $117,928 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Capital Projects Professional & Technical Account to the Facility Renovation Stabilization Fund for continuing building design services.

item.title: File 26-1116 — Requesting approval of a transfer of $46,889.72 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Capital Projects Building I
item.description: Requesting approval of a transfer of $46,889.72 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Capital Projects Building Improvements Account to the Facility Renovation Stabilization Fund for continuing building design and construction services.

item.title: File 26-1117 — Requesting approval of a transfer of $185,357.58 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Engineering Capital Outlay
item.description: Requesting approval of a transfer of $185,357.58 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management-Engineering Capital Outlay Engineering & Architecture Account to the Street Reconstruction Stabilization Fund for continuing street, sidewalk, and pavement condition assessment and design services.

item.title: File 26-1122 — Requesting approval of a transfer of $74,670.64 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management Capital Outlay Professional
item.description: Requesting approval of a transfer of $74,670.64 from the Infrastructure and Asset Management Capital Outlay Professional & Technical Account to the Facility Renovation Stabilization Fund for continuing asset management program and planning services.

item.title: File 26-1118 — Requesting that this Council convene in Executive Session to discuss item #26-1109, a request for appropriation to the L
item.description: Requesting that this Council convene in Executive Session to discuss item #26-1109, a request for appropriation to the Law Department Judgments & Settlements Account.

item.title: File 26-1119 — City Attorney conveying an update regarding item #26-1025.
item.description: City Attorney conveying an update regarding item #26-1025.

item.title: File 26-1120 — School Committee Chair conveying the Fiscal Year 2027 School Department budget as approved by the School Committee on Ju
item.description: School Committee Chair conveying the Fiscal Year 2027 School Department budget as approved by the School Committee on June 8, 2026.

item.description: Virtual

item.title: 2026-06-22 — Traffic and Parking Committee
item.description: Virtual

item.title: File 26-1110 — Requesting the appropriation of $201,495 from the Unreserved Fund Balance ("Free Cash") to the Judgments & Settlements A
item.description: Requesting the appropriation of $201,495 from the Unreserved Fund Balance ("Free Cash") to the Judgments & Settlements Account for settlement payments relative to the purchase of the Assembly Square Fire Station at 45 Middlesex Avenue.
